persona_name: Alex and Jordan — Craft Beer and Hiking Couple

ux_issues.description: Iceland itinerary initial creation only produced 1 generic item ('Laugavegur Trail Hike') instead of a day-by-day breakdown. Required a follow-up message to get the full 7-day itinerary. The Czech trip correctly created 12 items on the first ask.
ux_issues.suggestion: When the AI has enough context (trail name, duration, bookend city), it should proactively create a multi-day itinerary on first request rather than requiring a second prompt.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items twice in the same turn for the Czech itinerary — first creating 12 items, then attempting the same 12 again (correctly deduped as already_exists). This wastes a tool loop iteration.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Step 3a planning message for Czech beer itinerary

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI called create_itinerary_items during companion mode (adding Lokal Dlouhaaa to itinerary) without being asked to add it. In companion mode, itinerary additions should only fire on explicit 'add to my plan' requests.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Companion mode message asking for beer recommendation near Old Town Square

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated impressive depth on both extended location profiles (CZ and IS) and niche themes (craft-beer and hiking). Czech beer recommendations included specific venues like Pivovarsky dum, Zly Casy, U Fleku, and used proper Czech terminology (tmave pivo, hladinka, snyt, svetly lezak). The Laugavegur Trail itinerary correctly mapped the 4 segments (Landmannalaugar to Hrafntinnusker, Hrafntinnusker to Alftavatn, etc.) and mentioned hut booking urgency. The two-trip workflow worked flawlessly — trips stayed completely independent. Extended profiles are loading correctly. Minor issues: Iceland itinerary required a follow-up to get full detail, and the AI made unnecessary duplicate tool calls.
